NKJV.1: A Psalm of David. I will praise You with my whole heart; Before the gods I will sing praises to You.
NKJV.2: I will worship toward Your holy temple, And praise Your name For Your lovingkindness and Your truth; For You have magnified Your word above all Your name.
NKJV.3: In the day when I cried out, You answered me, And made me bold with strength in my soul.
NKJV.4: All the kings of the earth shall praise You, O LORD, When they hear the words of Your mouth.
NKJV.5: Yes, they shall sing of the ways of the LORD, For great is the glory of the LORD.
NKJV.6: Though the LORD is on high, Yet He regards the lowly; But the proud He knows from afar.
NKJV.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, You will revive me; You will stretch out Your hand Against the wrath of my enemies, And Your right hand will save me.
NKJV.8: The LORD will perfect that which concerns me; Your mercy, O LORD, endures forever; Do not forsake the works of Your hands.

ESV.1: I give you thanks, O LORD, with my whole heart; before the gods I sing your praise;
ESV.2: I bow down toward your holy temple and give thanks to your name for your steadfast love and your faithfulness, for you have exalted above all things your name and your word.
ESV.3: On the day I called, you answered me; my strength of soul you increased.
ESV.4: All the kings of the earth shall give you thanks, O LORD, for they have heard the words of your mouth,
ESV.5: and they shall sing of the ways of the LORD, for great is the glory of the LORD.
ESV.6: For though the LORD is high, he regards the lowly, but the haughty he knows from afar.
ESV.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, you preserve my life; you stretch out your hand against the wrath of my enemies, and your right hand delivers me.
ESV.8: The LORD will fulfill his purpose for me; your steadfast love, O LORD, endures forever. Do not forsake the work of your hands.

NASB.1: [A Psalm] of David. I will give You thanks with all my heart; I will sing praises to You before the gods.
NASB.2: I will bow down toward Your holy temple And give thanks to Your name for Your lovingkindness and Your truth; For You have magnified Your word according to all Your name.
NASB.3: On the day I called, You answered me; You made me bold with strength in my soul.
NASB.4: All the kings of the earth will give thanks to You, O LORD, When they have heard the words of Your mouth.
NASB.5: And they will sing of the ways of the LORD, For great is the glory of the LORD.
NASB.6: For though the LORD is exalted, Yet He regards the lowly, But the haughty He knows from afar.
NASB.7: Though I walk in the midst of trouble, You will revive me; You will stretch forth Your hand against the wrath of my enemies, And Your right hand will save me.
NASB.8: The LORD will accomplish what concerns me; Your lovingkindness, O LORD, is everlasting; Do not forsake the works of Your hands.

NLT.1:
I give you thanks, O LORD, with all my heart;
I will sing your praises before the gods.
NLT.2:
I bow before your holy Temple as I worship.
I praise your name for your unfailing love and faithfulness;
for your promises are backed
by all the honor of your name.
NLT.3:
As soon as I pray, you answer me;
you encourage me by giving me strength.
NLT.4:
Every king in all the earth will thank you, LORD,
for all of them will hear your words.
NLT.5:
Yes, they will sing about the LORD’s ways,
for the glory of the LORD is very great.
NLT.6:
Though the LORD is great, he cares for the humble,
but he keeps his distance from the proud.
NLT.7:
Though I am surrounded by troubles,
you will protect me from the anger of my enemies.
You reach out your hand,
and the power of your right hand saves me.
NLT.8:
The LORD will work out his plans for my life —
for your faithful love, O LORD, endures forever.
Don’t abandon me, for you made me.

WEB.1: By David. I will give you thanks with my whole heart. Before the gods, I will sing praises to you.
WEB.2: I will bow down toward your holy temple, and give thanks to your Name for your loving kindness and for your truth; for you have exalted your Name and your Word above all.
WEB.3: In the day that I called, you answered me. You encouraged me with strength in my soul.
WEB.4: All the kings of the earth will give you thanks, Yahweh, for they have heard the words of your mouth.
WEB.5: Yes, they will sing of the ways of Yahweh; for great is Yahweh’s glory.
WEB.6: For though Yahweh is high, yet he looks after the lowly; but the proud, he knows from afar.
WEB.7: Though I walk in the middle of trouble, you will revive me. You will stretch out your hand against the wrath of my enemies. Your right hand will save me.
WEB.8: Yahweh will fulfill that which concerns me; your loving kindness, Yahweh, endures forever. Don’t forsake the works of your own hands.

CSB17.1:
Of David. [1] I will give you thanks with all my heart; ⓓ
I will sing your praise before the heavenly beings. [1] ⓔ
CSB17.2:
I will bow down toward your holy temple
and give thanks to your name
for your constant love and truth.
You have exalted your name
and your promise above everything else. [2] ⓕ
CSB17.3:
On the day I called, you answered me;
you increased strength within me. [3] ⓖ
CSB17.4:
All the kings on earth will give you thanks, LORD,
when they hear what you have promised. [4] ⓗ
CSB17.5:
They will sing of the LORD’s ways,
for the LORD’s glory is great. ⓘ
CSB17.6:
Though the LORD is exalted,
he takes note of the humble;
but he knows the haughty from a distance. ⓙ
CSB17.7:
If I walk into the thick of danger,
you will preserve my life
from the anger of my enemies. ⓚ
You will extend your hand;
your right hand will save me. ⓛ
CSB17.8:
The LORD will fulfill his purpose for me. ⓜ
LORD, your faithful love endures forever;
do not abandon the work of your hands. ⓝ

AMP.1:
A Psalm of David. I will give You thanks with all my heart;
I sing praises to You before the [pagan] gods.
AMP.2:
I will bow down [in worship] toward Your holy temple
And give thanks to Your name for Your lovingkindness and Your truth;
For You have magnified Your word together with Your name.
AMP.3:
On the day I called, You answered me;
And You made me bold and confident with [renewed] strength in my life.
AMP.4:
All the kings of the land will give thanks and praise You, O LORD,
When they have heard of the promises of Your mouth [which were fulfilled].
AMP.5:
Yes, they will sing of the ways of the LORD [joyfully celebrating His wonderful acts],
For great is the glory and majesty of the LORD.
AMP.6:
Though the LORD is exalted,
He regards the lowly [and invites them into His fellowship];
But the proud and haughty He knows from a distance.
AMP.7:
Though I walk in the midst of trouble, You will revive me;
You will stretch out Your hand against the wrath of my enemies,
And Your right hand will save me.
AMP.8:
The LORD will accomplish that which concerns me;
Your [unwavering] lovingkindness, O LORD, endures forever—
Do not abandon the works of Your own hands.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Thank you! Everything in me says "Thank you!"
Angels listen as I sing my thanks.
MSG.2:
I kneel in worship facing your holy temple
and say it again: "Thank you!"
Thank you for your love,
thank you for your faithfulness;
Most holy is your name,
most holy is your Word.
MSG.3:
The moment I called out, you stepped in;
you made my life large with strength.
MSG.4:
When they hear what you have to say, God,
all earth's kings will say "Thank you."
MSG.5:
They'll sing of what you've done:
"How great the glory of God!"
MSG.6:
And here's why: God, high above, sees far below;
no matter the distance, he knows everything about us.
MSG.7:
When I walk into the thick of trouble,
keep me alive in the angry turmoil.
With one hand
strike my foes,
With your other hand
save me.
MSG.8:
Finish what you started in me, God.
Your love is eternal—don't quit on me now.

NIV.1:
Psalm 138
Of David.
I will praise you, O Lord, with all my heart;
before the “gods” I will sing your praise.
NIV.2:
I will bow down toward your holy temple
and will praise your name
for your love and your faithfulness,
for you have exalted above all things
your name and your word.
NIV.3:
When I called, you answered me;
you made me bold and stouthearted.
NIV.4:
May all the kings of the earth praise you, O Lord,
when they hear the words of your mouth.
NIV.5:
May they sing of the ways of the Lord,
for the glory of the Lord is great.
NIV.6:
Though the Lord is on high, he looks upon the lowly,
but the proud he knows from afar.
NIV.7:
Though I walk in the midst of trouble,
you preserve my life;
you stretch out your hand against the anger of my foes,
with your right hand you save me.
NIV.8:
The Lord will fulfill his purpose for me;
your love, O Lord, endures forever—
do not abandon the works of your hands.

NRSVCE.1:
I give you thanks, O LORD, with my whole heart;
before the gods I sing your praise;
NRSVCE.2:
I bow down toward your holy temple
and give thanks to your name for your steadfast love and your faithfulness;
for you have exalted your name and your word
above everything.
NRSVCE.3:
On the day I called, you answered me,
you increased my strength of soul.
NRSVCE.4:
All the kings of the earth shall praise you, O LORD,
for they have heard the words of your mouth.
NRSVCE.5:
They shall sing of the ways of the LORD,
for great is the glory of the LORD.
NRSVCE.6:
For though the LORD is high, he regards the lowly;
but the haughty he perceives from far away.
NRSVCE.7:
Though I walk in the midst of trouble,
you preserve me against the wrath of my enemies;
you stretch out your hand,
and your right hand delivers me.
NRSVCE.8:
The LORD will fulfill his purpose for me;
your steadfast love, O LORD, endures forever.
Do not forsake the work of your hands.

NET.1: I will give you thanks with all my heart; before the heavenly assembly I will sing praises to you.
NET.2: I will bow down toward your holy temple, and give thanks to your name, because of your loyal love and faithfulness, for you have exalted your promise above the entire sky.
NET.3: When I cried out for help, you answered me. You made me bold and energized me.
NET.4: Let all the kings of the earth give thanks to you, O LORD, when they hear the words you speak.
NET.5: Let them sing about the LORD’s deeds, for the LORD’s splendor is magnificent.
NET.6: Though the LORD is exalted, he takes note of the lowly, and recognizes the proud from far away.
NET.7: Even when I must walk in the midst of danger, you revive me. You oppose my angry enemies, and your right hand delivers me.
NET.8: The LORD avenges me. O LORD, your loyal love endures. Do not abandon those whom you have made!
